items.title: Art and Resistance on the Nation’s 250th
items.summary: Our stories this week are a testament to the people’s power, and a love letter to creative communities.

items.title: Hopeful Art Fundraisers for Earthquake Relief in Venezuela
items.summary: Art sales featuring everything from paintings and prints to photographs, artist books, and zines are benefiting urgently needed aid efforts.

items.title: Required Reading
items.summary: This week: James Turrell in Denmark, a new album by Raven Chacon, a Black radical history of the Declaration of Independence, World Cup songs across time, and more.

items.title: New York Gallery Lyles & King to Close After 11 Years
items.summary: The gallery became known for championing the careers of Mira Schor, Chris Hood, Philip Birch, and Davina Semo, among others.
